prompt: I want to run simulation-based inference for image-based Bayesian denoising. You need to download the Fashion MNIST dataset usng keras (https://keras.io/api/datasets/fashion_mnist/). Treat the clean images as targets ('parameters') and the corresponding blurry images (you need to apply Gaussian blur) as conditions ('data'). Set up a BayesFlow workflow with proper handling of images as targets. Use a small subset of the train data as validation data.
assertions: Defines a BasicWorkflow without an explicit simulator
Uses the clean images as inference_variables and the noisy images as inference_conditions
Does NOT flatten any of the images
Does NOT use a summary network
Uses a diffusion model or flow matching with a UNet architecture as a subnet
Uses the fit_offline method of a BasicWorkflow
Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
Passes validation_data= to the fit_offline call
Saves and inspects training history (JSON + inspect_training or equivalent)
Does NOT run conventional in-silico diagnostics for low-dimensional problems
Creates and inspects a few posterior clean images for a few test examples
